info.title: OpenCompletions API
info.description:
A unified Next.js application that wraps AI coding CLIs as a completions and agent API, with OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic-compatible, and multi-turn Agent endpoints. Supports multiple execution backends (local, sprite, vercel) configured via the Settings page.

**Authentication**: All API endpoints require a Bearer token. API keys are created and managed on the Keys page. Include the key as `Authorization: Bearer <key>` or as the `x-api-key` header. The `x-api-key` header is also forwarded to the CLI backend as the provider auth token. Health check and docs endpoints require no authentication.

200.description: Chat completion response. When `stream: true`, returns SSE stream of `ChatCompletionChunk` objects.
Text completion response. When `stream: true`, returns SSE stream.
Message response. When `stream: true`, returns SSE stream of Anthropic event types.

schema.description: Anthropic SSE events: message_start, content_block_start, content_block_delta, content_block_stop, message_delta, message_stop

input_tokens.description: Estimated input token count (~4 chars per token)

post.description: Returns deterministic hash-based pseudo-embeddings (1536 dimensions). These are NOT semantically meaningful — they exist for SDK compatibility with tools like Continue.dev and LangChain that expect this endpoint. Use a real embedding provider for semantic search.
OpenAI's new unified API format. Accepts `input` (string or message array) and optional `instructions` (system prompt). Supports streaming.

200.description: Response object. When `stream: true`, returns SSE with response.* event types.

schema.description: SSE events: response.created, response.output_text.delta, response.completed, etc.

post.summary: Run a multi-turn agent with tool use
post.description: Starts an autonomous agent that can use tools (Read, Edit, Bash, etc.) across multiple turns. Streams Claude Code's NDJSON events as SSE by default. Supports session resumption, tool restrictions, MCP servers, and budget caps.

200.description: Agent run result. When `stream: true` (default), returns SSE with event types: `system`, `assistant`, `user`, `result`, `done`. When `stream: false`, returns a single JSON object.

get.description: Returns the default backend and all available backends with their status and pool details.
Returns a paginated list of agent runs, optionally filtered by key ID, org ID, or status.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of runs to return

get.description: Returns aggregate statistics about agent runs, optionally filtered by key, org, or time range. Also includes current worker and queue status.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Unix timestamp (ms) — only count runs after this time

200.description: Current queue depth and worker status

post.summary: Upload a file to a workspace
post.description: Uploads a file to a workspace. The filename is specified via the `X-Filename` header. Optionally specify `X-Workspace-Id` to upload to an existing workspace, otherwise a new workspace is created.

parameters.name: X-Workspace-Id
parameters.description: Existing workspace ID to upload into

200.description: List of files in the workspace

delete.summary: Delete a workspace and all its files

get.summary: Download a file from a workspace

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The filename (may include path separators for nested files)

BearerAuth.description: API key created on the Keys page. Include as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

ProviderKey.name: x-api-key
ProviderKey.description: Provider auth token (e.g. Anthropic OAuth or API key) forwarded to the CLI backend per-request.

stream.description: If true, returns SSE stream of chunks

suffix.description: Suffix text for fill-in-the-middle (FIM) completions. Appended after the generated completion.

prompt.description: The task for the agent to perform

session_id.description: Resume a previous agent session (requires same backend worker)

max_budget_usd.description: Cost cap in USD. Agent stops when exceeded.

allowed_tools.description: Whitelist of tools the agent may use (e.g. ["Read", "Glob", "Grep"])

disallowed_tools.description: Blacklist of tools the agent may not use

model.description: Override the Claude model used by the agent

cwd.description: Working directory for the agent (local backend only)

stream.description: If true (default), returns SSE event stream. If false, returns buffered JSON.

mcp_servers.description: Per-request MCP server configuration. Keys are server names, values are server configs.

AgentSystemEvent.description: Initial event with session info and available tools

AgentAssistantEvent.description: Assistant message (may contain text and/or tool_use blocks)

AgentResultEvent.description: Final result event with cost and usage summary

result.description: Final text output from the agent

session_id.description: Session ID for resuming this agent

result.description: Final text output from the agent

num_turns.description: Number of turns the agent took

events.description: All events that occurred during the agent run

StatsResponse.description: Aggregate run statistics plus current server status

input.description: The input to generate a response for. Can be a string or array of message objects.

stream.description: If true, returns SSE stream of response events

tags.name: OpenAI-Compatible
tags.description: Endpoints compatible with the OpenAI API format

tags.name: Anthropic-Compatible
tags.description: Endpoints compatible with the Anthropic API format

tags.name: Agent
tags.description: Multi-turn autonomous agent API with tool use and MCP support

tags.name: Files
tags.description: Workspace file management — upload, list, download, and delete

tags.name: Dashboard
tags.description: Agent run history, statistics, and monitoring

tags.name: Utility
tags.description: Health check, status, and backend information
